# BI-260012: Integrate Google Analytics (GA4) on the public site

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260012
- Title: Add approved GA4 analytics integration to the public site
- Status: Done
- Persona/Actor: Repository owner / site operator
- Value statement: For the site operator, measure traffic and page engagement on
  the live public site so content and product decisions are informed by real usage.
- Context: ADR-260004 deferred analytics past the initial launch. The site is now
  live and the deferred "later phase" has arrived; ADR-260012 approves the
  exception that ADR-260004's rollback clause anticipated.
- Problem statement: Without analytics there is no visibility into how visitors
  reach and move through the public site.
- Expected outcome: GA4 is loaded on all content pages using the official gtag.js
  snippet, sending page_view hits to property `G-CS37QHFW49`.
- Success metric(s): GA4 Realtime shows page_view hits from a clean browser; the
  `collect` request returns 204.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - The official gtag.js snippet is present in the `<head>` of every content page,
    rendered from a single shared `Analytics.astro` component.
  - The snippet is used verbatim (no rest-params rewrite), per LRN-002.
  - Redirect-only stubs (`/landing`, `/gaddon`) need not carry the tag.
  - The form-backend exclusion and all other ADR-260004 boundaries remain enforced.
  - Boundary tests assert the GA tag is present on content pages.
- Non-goals: Cookie-consent banner, custom event tracking, or alternative
  (self-hosted) analytics vendors — deferred to future items if needed.
- Constraints: Static hosting; the snippet must remain a client-side inline script.
- Dependencies: ADR-260012 approval.
- Risks/assumptions: Privacy/compliance scope may later require a consent
  mechanism; ad-blockers/private DNS will under-count own-traffic (see LRN-002).
- Validation approach: Automated boundary tests plus manual GA4 Realtime check
  from a clean browser.
- Priority score: Medium
- Target window: Post-launch
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260004.md (analytics exception approved via ADR-260012)
  - ADR: ADR-260012.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
  - Related learning: ../learnings/LRN-002-google-analytics-ga4-setup.md
